geopolitics_summary: EU-UK emissions trading deal postponed; Asia-Europe LNG competition intensifying; EU GHG emissions rose 2025.

themes.headline: EU-UK emissions trading agreement talks delayed by UK political crisis
themes.why_it_matters: ETS linkage with UK market affects EU carbon price formation and arbitrage opportunity windows. Postponement reduces near-term regulatory clarity on cross-border trading mechanics; watch for rescheduled summit date.

themes.headline: Europe faces rising LNG bidding competition from China, Asia markets
themes.why_it_matters: Increased Asian LNG demand narrows European supply access and raises TTF spot/forward prices. Direct transmission to EU power via fuel-switching economics and winter gas adequacy risk.

themes.headline: EU greenhouse gas emissions increased in 2025, carbon credit supply pressured
themes.why_it_matters: Rising emissions imply higher cap-and-trade demand; potential upward pressure on EUA futures. Stalled decarbonisation may trigger stricter Phase 4 auction schedules or MSR drawdowns, tightening carbon cost assumptions in power generation dispatch.

themes.headline: Data center operators lobby for gas-fired baseload to meet EU AI demand
themes.why_it_matters: Sector argues renewable grids insufficient for AI load growth; signals structural demand for gas peaking/baseload capacity. Upward pressure on gas-fired plant utilisation and natural gas short-curve forwards (Cal+1, Q+1).

themes.headline: Fossil fuel industry pushes back on EU methane emissions reduction rules
themes.why_it_matters: Methane regulation affects upstream supply costs and LNG import economics. Potential lobby success could defer compliance capex, easing near-term gas production but clouding longer-term supply-cost pass-through to TTF.

watchlist: European Council trade/energy agenda closure (mid-late June 2026).
EU-UK summit rescheduling for emissions trading linkage finalisation.
Q3 2026 EU ETS auction calendar and MSR weekly submission trends.
LNG import nominations vs. Asian demand signals (July onwards).
